avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New fresh unstable windslab will develop mainly on North to East to aspects above 800 metres. Minor amounts may also be encountered on both North-West and South-East aspect due to cross loading. Steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops will be most affected, particularly where accumulations develop over old snow. Cornice stability will remain poor. Old snow will become very firm and icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Precipitation is forecast throughout, heaviest overnight when the freezing level will be just above the summits. By day the freezing level will drop with snowfall easing but falling to around 600 metres. Storm force winds overnight easing to strong gale force.
Observed Weather Influences: Limited snowfall overnight followed by a dry and sunny day. The freezing level lifted to around +2 at 900 metres by afternoon. Strong gale force winds, Westerly throughout the day.
ObservedSnowStability: New snowfall was largely cosmetic. The old course snow was softening in the sun at lower levels but overall remains firm and well bonded in all locations. Cover is still reasonable above 850 metres on West through North to East aspects. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Poor stability overnight and again from dawn onwards. Weak windslab will become deeper and more extensive on all steep North to East aspects above 950m. Weak newly formed cornices anticipated above these locations. Better stability at lower altitudes, on ridges and all wind exposed aspects.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Stormy and wintry weather in prospect. A sequence of heavy snow showers throughout the period easing a little after midday. Rain at lower altitudes for a time. Very strong and gusty South South West winds becoming South West later.
Observed Weather Influences: Wet and mild overnight then colder with frequent, punchy snow showers. Very strong and blustery West South Westerly airflow.
ObservedSnowStability: Thin cover of fresh moist snow down to 750m in all locations. Persistent drifting induced the development of new moderately bonded windslab on steep North to East aspects. New accumulations were localised at the time of reporting, only gaining significant depth above 1000m. Some new minor cornice development noted above these places. Good stability in all other locations.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow will form  unstable windslab in wind sheltered locations. Greatest accumulations will be in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect mainly above 800 metres. Fragile cornices may form in these areas. Wind exposed slopes will firm and icy in places with good general stability.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: New snow will affect slopes mainly above 900 metres overnight but lowering to around 400 metres through the course of the day. Gale to storm force South-Westerly winds overnight will ease a touch through the day but staying strong to gale force. The freezing level will briefly rise above the summits overnight lowering to 500 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: New snow affected slopes mainly above 800 metres, winds have been generally South-Westerly strong to gale force with occasional stronger gusts. The freezing level is around 950 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable areas of windslab have formed in wind sheltered locations mainly above 900 metres. Areas most affected are sheltered gully exits and on steep corrie headwalls, these areas are generally avoidable. Elsewhere the strong winds have scoured exposed slopes which are becoming firm and icy in places. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop through the period mainly on North-West through North to East aspects above 1000 metres. For a time overnight there will be wet snow instabilities on these aspects between 900 and 1200 metres. Fragile cornices will be present above these aspects. The older snowpack will continue to have good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Storm force South-South-Westerly winds overnight veer to the South-West and reduce to gale force during the day. Precipitation, heaviest in the evening and morning, with the snow rain limit around 750 metres in the evening, rising to 1100 metres overnight and then gradually dropping to 400 metres by the end of the afternoon. The freezing level will rise to 1200 metres for a time overnight before dropping to 500 metres by the end of the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: There were gale force West-South-Westerly winds and showers falling as snow above 750 metres. The freezing level was around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of fresh moderately bonded windslab are present mainly on North to East aspects above 950 metres. Fragile cornices are present above these aspects. The older snowpack has mainly frozen in the cooler temperatures and has good stability. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will continue to develop on North-West through North to East aspects mainly above 1000 metres. Areas most affected will be coire rims, gully tops and other steep wind sheltered locations. Below this height, localised instabilities will be present but these are not expected to be extensive. Fragile cornices will be present on the same aspects. Many areas will be wind scoured with the old snowpack remaining generally stable throughout. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Storm force South-Westerly winds will continue through the evening before easing to gale force around dawn. Fresh snowfall expected initially as low at 700 metres before being confined to the highest summits when heaviest snow accumulation is expected. Snowfall will continue through the day down to around 600 metres by the afternoon. The freezing level will rise to 1200 metres overnight before lowering down to around 700 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Storm force winds from the West-South-West eased slightly during the afternoon. Heavy squally snow showers throughout the day accompanied with sunny intervals. The freezing level was around 900 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Instabilities within the snowpack are present on North through East to South-East aspects above 850 metres. Areas most affected are coire rims, gully tops and other steep wind sheltered locations. Cornices remain present on the same aspects. The old snowpack has started to refreeze with generally good stability throughout. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Localised deposits of moderately bonded windslab will develop through the day. Steep wind sheltered locations on North to East aspects above 750 metres will be most affected. These deposits will generally be shallow and often overlie bare ground. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Rain at all levels overnight will turn to snow on the summits from around dawn, falling as snow above around 500 metres by the end of the afternoon as the freezing level falls. Very strong South-westerly winds overnight will ease very slightly during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight rain turned to snow showers above around 700 metres which eased to give a mostly drier period in the middle of the day and further snow showers towards the end of the afternoon. Very strong Westerly winds throughout the period.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing patchy snow cover remains well bonded and stable. A superficial covering of new snow is present above around 750 metres, with slightly greater amounts on the more inland hills than the coastal hills. The avalanche hazard is Low.
